what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that records three under-specified platform and operations realities that the project brief has not costed, as section 8 of a larger P10 item.
observations: The item is linked as part_of board://item-eb5d71e7 and sits in the dragnet-development channel.
Title is 'P10 · 8. Quiet platform/ops realities the brief never costs'.
First bullet describes Tailscale-only HTTPS to a workstation (CTX1) and the need for offline tolerance, optimistic UI, and a draft queue that survives a dropped tunnel.
It notes this implies real local persistence (IndexedDB) plus a sync/reconcile subsystem.
It flags a bad interaction with 'mark-is-truth' when a locally queued draft reconnects to a board that moved underneath it.
Second bullet frames 'invitations / codes' to spin up Claude Code members as session-spawn / clone with identity.
It cites Tim's memory rule 'autonomous-spawn-lead-only' that sub-agents must NOT fire 'claude -p' autonomously, leaving the feature unresolved.

Third bullet highlights a connector-client identity gap: an external client currently has no session, and routing replies back to Tim's phone overlay from an arbitrary member assumes an unsettled return path due to the three-channel split (B4).
standouts: A proposed product feature ('phone mints a code that spawns an acting agent') directly collides with a standing safety rule.
The return-path problem for external clients is explicitly left unresolved because the channel split has not been finalized.
The local-draft / mark-is-truth reconciliation risk is called out as a cross-cutting subsystem concern.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a Markdown body with a numbered section heading and bullet list.
References external brief identifiers (BD-F, BD-E, B4) and a person-specific rule without defining them in the file.
Uses board:// URIs for inter-item links.
concerns: The item records unresolved conflicts but offers no proposed resolution or owner.
It relies on abbreviations and external context (CTX1, BD-F, BD-E, B4, Tim's rule) that are not explained locally.
It bundles networking, identity/routing, and agent-governance concerns into a single item, which may obscure tracking.
notes: A noticeboard item surfacing three un-costed platform/operations gaps—offline sync, agent-spawn policy conflict, and client identity/routing—that need design resolution.
questions: What is the exact definition and enforcement of 'mark-is-truth' and how should it handle queued drafts that conflict with remote board state?
What are the details of the three-channel split (B4) and why has it not settled the return path for external clients?
What is the full text and exception policy of Tim's 'autonomous-spawn-lead-only' rule?
What is the intended user-facing behavior when a locally queued draft is applied after the board has changed underneath it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to list quiet platform/ops realities the brief never costs, and the content matches by enumerating three operational gaps and their implications.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to surface hidden operational complexity and policy conflicts that could derail implementation if they remain unaddressed in the brief.
novelty: It explicitly names a standing safety rule that blocks a proposed feature, and it connects a network topology constraint to a data-model reconciliation risk.
contribution: It records three specific operational and identity risks along with their cross-cutting dependencies, which a fused system would keep as input to requirements and architecture decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel listing three un-costed platform and operations realities—Tailscale-only workstation access requiring offline sync, invitation-based agent spawning conflicting with a no-autonomous-launch rule, and an unsettled return path for external client replies—related to a larger brief.
intention_for_embedding: To surface unresolved platform, identity, and agent-governance gaps that the project brief omitted so they can be addressed before implementation.
